sentence_reviews.text: Beam search retains a bounded set of high-scoring partial sequences, optionally applying lexical, grammar, length, or domain constraints during decoding.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA search background and DL sequence-decoding background; constrained-decoding details are synthesized from the decoding concept rather than directly cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: Bounded high-scoring partial sequences are directly characteristic of beam search; the constraint examples broaden the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Beam search & constrained decoding is to connect Search, sampling & decoding procedures, Autoregressive decoding to downstream capabilities including Code models, copilots & repository intelligence, Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Search and sequence-decoding background; downstream code and speech applications are synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source support is general for search/decoding, not the exact downstream ontology phrasing.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Code models, copilots & repository intelligence
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sequence modeling and decoding background; code-model-specific evidence is not cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: Beam/constrained decoding can be used for code generation, but the packet does not directly cite code-model evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding
sentence_reviews.notes: Beam search is a standard decoding procedure in sequence tasks, but the packet evidence is general rather than speech-specific.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Search, sampling & decoding procedures, Autoregressive decoding.
sentence_reviews.notes: Extra search cost is supported; confident-error and verifier language is broader than the cited sources.

sentence_reviews.text: Beam search & constrained decoding explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Search, sampling & decoding procedures.
sentence_reviews.notes: Beam search directly explores and scores alternative partial sequences.

sentence_reviews.text: Beam search & constrained decoding explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Autoregressive decoding.
sentence_reviews.notes: Beam search is commonly used with autoregressive decoders, though constrained decoding can also appear in other sequence settings.

sentence_reviews.text: Beam search & constrained decoding explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA constraint/search background; constrained decoding linkage is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: Constrained decoding may use constraint mechanisms, but this is not established as a common dependency by the packet sources.

edge_reviews.notes: Beam search is directly a search/decoding procedure.
Core for many neural sequence-decoding uses, but not every beam search use requires autoregressive decoding.
Constraint mechanisms can support constrained decoding, but the packet evidence does not clearly support the edge strength.
